summary: Cosmic Charlie jam shines; tight Wheel transition and strong second set
blurb: The show delivers a stellar Playing > Cosmic Charlie sequence, with Jerry’s guitar soaring on Scarlet Begonias and The Wheel. Phil adds bold bass moments while the dual drummers lock in tight grooves. Highlights include Cosmic Charlie, High Time, and an inspired second‑set flow.
review: On July 16, 1976 at San Francisco’s historic Orpheum Theatre the Dead served up one of their most rewarding mid‑70s outings. The first set rolls steadily through classics – Cold Rain And Snow, Deal, and a bright Scarlet Begonias that segues into The Music Never Stopped – but it’s the second set that truly steals the show. A blistering Playing In The Band launches a seamless jam that morphs into Stronger Than Dirt Or Milkin' The Turkey before exploding into an unforgettable Cosmic Charlie. From there the band rides a jazzy wave through Samson & Delilah, Spanish Jam and a tight Wheel transition back into Playin’, culminating in a joyous High Time and Sugar Magnolia encore. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout – his Travis Bean tone gleams on Scarlet and his solo on Brown Eyed Women is a highlight – while Phil’s daring 7/4 bass riff hints at experimental ambition, even if it never fully lands. Keith’s piano flourishes and Donna Jean’s vocal warmth add texture, and Mickey and Bill keep the rhythmic foundation rock‑solid. The recording landscape is rich: an excellent soundboard capture (the recommended version) preserves the clarity of the jam while an audience bleed adds live ambience, making this a must‑listen for fans who appreciate both musical tightness and that classic Orpheum energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All eight recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the Playing > Cosmic Charlie sequence, Jerry’s lead work, and tight second‑set flow. Multiple sources highlight the same standout songs (Cosmic Charlie, The Wheel, High Time) and commend the dual drummers’ groove, indicating strong consensus of excellence.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard with subtle audience bleed captures the Playing > Cosmic Charlie jam clearly and preserves full band dynamics without dropouts.

key_highlights: Playing In The Band → Stronger Than Dirt → Cosmic Charlie sequence shines
Tight transition from The Wheel back into Playin' keeps momentum high
Jerry’s Travis Bean tone on Scarlet Begonias and soaring solo in Brown Eyed Women
Dual drummers lock in a flawless groove throughout the second set
song_highlights: Stronger Than Dirt Or Milkin' The Turkey

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar work is consistently praised – lyrical phrasing, gleaming Travis Bean tone on Scarlet Begonias and an outstanding solo in Brown Eyed Women; he weaves classic ’75 style licks through the jams.
band_performance.Phil: Provides bold bass moments, notably a daring 7/4 riff during the Stronger Than Dirt section and punchy lines that drive the jam, though his mix can be high or occasionally buried depending on source.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar and vocals are solid; his work on The Wheel is highlighted, contributing steady foundation without any notable missteps.
band_performance.Keith: Piano adds tasteful flourishes, especially during Looks Like Rain and the jam sections; slip‑knot‑like hints enrich the improvisations. Donna Jean’s vocal harmonies are noted as beautiful in the mix.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums lock tightly with Billy, delivering an exciting groove that propels the Playing > Cosmic Charlie sequence and keeps the second‑set momentum high.
band_performance.Billy: Shares a seamless rhythmic partnership with Mickey; his contributions during the drum segment are described as thrilling ('Whewww!!') and reinforce the band’s tightness.

must_listen_sequences: Stronger Than Dirt Or Milkin' The Turkey
